primary_factors: High-impact timing: rain overnight with temperatures falling below freezing around/just before bus-run time (6–8 AM), creating flash-freeze/black-ice risk even before the main freezing-rain window
Freezing rain risk 8–10 AM (school start window) + gusty winds: even a light glaze is a major Michigan closure trigger because it’s hard to mitigate on secondary/local roads and bridges
Safety assessment flags severe ice-formation and severe morning-commute impact; buses and teen drivers are the limiting factor, not snowfall totals
Infrastructure/treatment constraint: heavy rain can wash off pretreatment; effective re-treatment may not “hold” on secondary/local roads as temps crash, producing patchy, high-liability conditions
Power-grid risk is now meaningful: Kent County outages reported ~8.6k (~2.8%) and rising; forecast icing/wind could spike outages and prevent buildings/traffic signals from operating normally
decision_rationale:
Cross-referencing the experts, there is broad agreement that this is an ice/flash-freeze-timing event, not a snow-amount event. The main conflict is probability magnitude: meteorology is cautious (inconsistent model totals), while history/safety are higher due to known decision behavior around freezing rain at bus time. To resolve that, I weighted (1) the safety/infrastructure reality that heavy rain can wash off chemicals and produce sudden black ice as temps drop below freezing near 6–7 AM, (2) the explicit freezing-rain window around 8–10 AM, and (3) the updated power-grid signal showing Kent County outages already meaningful and increasing, with icing/wind likely to worsen them. 

Against that, news intelligence shows no confirmed neighboring-district closures yet; that’s expected 48 hours out and prevents me from going into the 85–95% ‘near-lock’ range today. With Michigan’s typically high bar for snow days, a full closure still becomes more likely than not when freezing rain overlaps bus and arrival times, especially with patchy secondary-road icing and rising outages. Net: I land at 74% for a full closure, with a substantial remaining chance that districts choose a 2-hour delay if the changeover/icing shifts later or is less intense than depicted.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 14.0% spread. Average probability: 66%.
